source.reference: Two-stage flash cascade for benzene/toluene. Stage 1 isothermal flash at high pressure, liquid product re-flashed at reduced pressure. Based on standard VLE separation principles from Seader/Henley 'Separation Process Principles'.
source.verification_note: v0.1.0: Expected values verified by Brent's method on Rachford-Rice equation using Antoine constants from Perry's (log10, mmHg, °C). Both stages verified independently: RR residual < 1e-12, material balance closes exactly across all stages, overall benzene balance: 30.921 + 10.775 + 18.304 = 60.000 kmol/h.

problem.statement:
A benzene/toluene feed (60 mol% benzene, 40 mol% toluene) at 100 kmol/h enters a two-stage flash separation cascade.

Stage 1: The feed is flashed at T = 365 K and P = 101.325 kPa (1 atm). The vapor product (V1) is collected.

Stage 2: The liquid product from Stage 1 is sent to a second flash drum operating at T = 350 K and P = 60 kPa. The vapor product (V2) is collected, and the liquid product (L2) is the bottoms stream.

Determine the compositions and flow rates at each stage, and the overall recovery of benzene in the combined vapor products (V1 + V2).
problem.task: Calculate: (1) Stage 1 vapor fraction, liquid and vapor benzene mole fractions, and flow rates. (2) Stage 2 vapor fraction, liquid and vapor benzene mole fractions, and flow rates. (3) Overall benzene recovery in the combined vapor products as a percentage of benzene in the feed.

feed_flow_rate.description: Total molar flow rate of fresh feed

feed_composition_benzene.description: Mole fraction of benzene in feed

feed_composition_toluene.description: Mole fraction of toluene in feed

antoine_benzene.description: Antoine coefficients [A, B, C] for benzene: log10(P/mmHg) = A - B/(C + T/°C)

antoine_toluene.description: Antoine coefficients [A, B, C] for toluene: log10(P/mmHg) = A - B/(C + T/°C)

stage1_vapor_fraction.description: Fraction of feed vaporized in Stage 1 (V1/F)

stage1_liquid_benzene.description: Mole fraction of benzene in Stage 1 liquid

stage1_vapor_benzene.description: Mole fraction of benzene in Stage 1 vapor

stage2_vapor_fraction.description: Fraction of Stage 2 feed (= L1) vaporized

stage2_liquid_benzene.description: Mole fraction of benzene in Stage 2 liquid (final bottoms)

stage2_vapor_benzene.description: Mole fraction of benzene in Stage 2 vapor

stage2_liquid_flow.description: Stage 2 liquid product flow rate (final bottoms)

overall_benzene_recovery.description: Percentage of feed benzene recovered in combined vapor products (V1 + V2)

stage2_vapor_fraction._comment: Wider tolerance — errors compound from Stage 1

stage2_liquid_benzene._comment: Wider tolerance — errors compound from Stage 1

stage2_vapor_benzene._comment: Wider tolerance — errors compound from Stage 1

stage2_liquid_flow._comment: Within 3% — compound error from Stage 1 + Stage 2

overall_benzene_recovery._comment: Within 2 percentage points — integrates errors across both stages

must_include: Identify this as a multi-stage flash separation requiring sequential calculation
Use Raoult's law or appropriate VLE correlation at each stage
Recognize that Stage 2 feed composition equals Stage 1 liquid composition
Apply material balance at each stage and overall
State or use Antoine equation for vapor pressures
must_not_include: Treat the two stages as independent problems with the same feed
Use the original feed composition as Stage 2 feed composition
Ignore the pressure change between stages
Assume the same V/F ratio applies to both stages

domain_context.recommended_thermo_model: Raoult's law (appropriate for benzene/toluene — nearly ideal system at moderate pressures)
key_assumptions: Ideal liquid mixture (benzene and toluene are chemically similar)
Thermal equilibrium at each flash drum
No heat exchange between stages — each drum operates at its specified temperature
common_mistakes: Feeding the original feed composition into Stage 2 instead of the Stage 1 liquid composition
Not recalculating K-values at the Stage 2 temperature and pressure
Accumulating rounding errors by truncating Stage 1 results before using them in Stage 2
Using wrong Antoine constant units (check ln vs log10, Pa vs mmHg, K vs °C)
Forgetting that V/F is unknown at both stages (T and P are given — this is a T,P-flash)
Not verifying overall material balance across both stages

reasoning_checkpoints.checkpoint: Identifies problem as a multi-stage (sequential) flash requiring chained calculations
Correctly solves Stage 1 flash: calculates K-values, applies Rachford-Rice, finds V/F
Uses Stage 1 liquid composition as Stage 2 feed composition (not original feed)
Recalculates K-values at Stage 2 conditions (different T and P)
Correctly solves Stage 2 flash with propagated intermediate results
Computes overall benzene recovery from both vapor streams
Verifies overall material balance: F = L2 + V1 + V2 and component balance closes
Manages numerical precision — does not truncate intermediate results prematurely
States assumptions explicitly (ideal mixture, steady state, etc.)
Organizes solution as a clear sequential computation (not a single monolithic equation)
